title: SugarCRM Documentation
prompt_source: Inline docs prompt; CONNECTOR_DOCUMENTATION_GUIDELINES fetched via getDocGuidelines()

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
**SugarCRM** is a CRM platform exposing a REST API (current major version v11; SugarCloud instances pin a sub-revision such as `v11_18`). This connector — `SugarCRMConnector` (`CONNECTOR_TYPE = "sugarcrm"`, `AUTH_TYPE = "oauth2_password"`) — wraps the operational CRM surfaces a Shielva tenant typically needs:

| Surface | Module path | Capability |
|---|---|---|
| Contacts | `/Contacts` | List · Get · Create · Update · Delete |
| Accounts | `/Accounts` | List (filtered) |
| Opportunities | `/Opportunities` | List · Create |
| Leads | `/Leads` | List · Convert |
| Meetings | `/Meetings` | List |
| Identity | `/me` | Health-check probe (lightweight) |

The connector normalises CRM records (Contacts, Accounts, Opportunities, Leads, Cases) into `NormalizedDocument` (id = `f"{tenant_id}_{source_id}"`), surfaces standalone `async def` methods per user-requested operation (OCP), retries 429/5xx with exponential backoff (3 attempts), and recovers from access-token expiry via a single-shot `401 → refresh → retry` wrapper (`helpers.utils.refresh_and_retry_on_401`). SOC — `connector.py` orchestrates only; all HTTP lives in `client/http_client.py::SugarCRMHTTPClient` and all record-shaping in `helpers/normalizer.py`.

sections.title: Quick Start
sections.content:
### 1. Identify the SugarCRM deployment

* **SugarCloud:** root URL is `https://<instance>.sugarondemand.com` (or `https://<instance>.sugarcrm.com`). The connector appends `/rest/v11` automatically.
* **On-prem (Sugar Sell / Serve / Enterprise):** root URL is your private SugarCRM hostname.

### 2. Provision a service account

1. Log in to SugarCRM as Administrator.
2. **Users → User Management → Create User** (regular user, not admin).
3. Set a strong password — this is what the connector authenticates with.
4. Assign the role that grants the minimum module access you intend to expose (Contacts/Accounts/Opportunities/etc.). The default `sugar` OAuth client only requires the **password** grant scope, which every user has by default.

### 3. Install in ACP

In **ACP → Manage Connectors → SugarCRM**, fill in:

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| `site_url` (text) | `https://yourcompany.sugarondemand.com` |
| `username` (text) | The service-account username from step 2 |
| `password` (secret) | The service-account password |
| `client_id` (text, optional) | Defaults to `sugar` — SugarCRM's built-in trusted client |
| `client_secret` (secret, optional) | Only required for **custom** OAuth applications |
| `grant_type` (text, optional) | Defaults to `password`. Set to `authorization_code` for SugarCloud OAuth apps |
| `platform` (text, optional) | Defaults to `api` |

Click **Install**. `install()` runs the OAuth2 password-grant token exchange immediately, stores the `access_token` + `refresh_token` via `set_token()`, and returns `InstallResult(success=True)` mapping to `HEALTHY + CONNECTED`.

### 4. First health check

The gateway calls `health_check()` which issues `GET /me` with the stored access token. On 2xx → `HEALTHY + CONNECTED`. On 401 → `DEGRADED + TOKEN_EXPIRED` (the connector also attempts an automatic refresh before reporting). On 403 → `UNHEALTHY + INVALID_CREDENTIALS`.

sections.title: Authentication
sections.content:
SugarCRM v11 uses OAuth2. This connector supports two grants:

### Password grant (default — on-prem + SugarCloud service accounts)

```
POST {site_url}/rest/v11/oauth2/token
Content-Type: application/json

{
  "grant_type":    "password",
  "client_id":     "sugar",
  "client_secret": "",
  "username":      "<service_account>",
  "password":      "<password>",
  "platform":      "api"
}
```

Response:

```json
{
  "access_token":  "...",
  "refresh_token": "...",
  "expires_in":    3600,
  "token_type":    "bearer"
}
```

### Authorization-code grant (SugarCloud OAuth apps)

1. The Shielva platform redirects the operator to `{site_url}/?module=OAuth2&action=authorize?response_type=code&client_id=<id>&redirect_uri=<uri>&state=<state>` via `connector.get_oauth_url(...)`.
2. Operator approves; SugarCRM redirects back with `?code=<auth_code>`.
3. The platform calls `connector.authorize(auth_code, state)` which POSTs `grant_type=authorization_code` to the token endpoint.

### Header contract

Every authenticated request:

```
OAuth-Token:  <access_token>     ← SugarCRM-specific (NOT 'Authorization: Bearer')
Content-Type: application/json
Accept:       application/json
```

### Refresh

`on_token_refresh()` runs:

1. If a `refresh_token` is on file → `POST /oauth2/token` with `grant_type=refresh_token`.
2. If that fails (401) **and** the connector still has `username` + `password` in config (password grant) → re-issue the password grant transparently so the gateway never sees `RefreshError` mid-flow.
3. Otherwise raise `RefreshError`.

A `401 → refresh → retry` wrapper sits around every authenticated call. The first 401 triggers a refresh + single retry; a second 401 in the same chain propagates.

sections.title: Configuration
sections.content:
### Install fields (per-tenant)

Read via `self.config.get(...)` in `connector.py::__init__` — never hardcoded, never from `os.environ`.

| Key | Type | Required | Default | Read in code | Purpose |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `site_url` | text | yes | — | `self.site_url` | SugarCRM root URL; `/rest/v11` appended automatically |
| `username` | text | yes (password grant) | — | `self.username` | Service-account username |
| `password` | secret | yes (password grant) | — | `self.password` | Service-account password (stored encrypted) |
| `client_id` | text | no | `sugar` | `self.client_id` | OAuth2 client id |
| `client_secret` | secret | no | `""` | `self.client_secret` | OAuth2 client secret (custom apps only) |
| `grant_type` | text | no | `password` | `self.grant_type` | `password` or `authorization_code` |
| `platform` | text | no | `api` | `self.platform` | SugarCRM `platform` string |
| `redirect_uri` | text | no | `""` | `self.config.get(...)` | Required when `grant_type=authorization_code` |
| `sync_modules` | array | no | `["Contacts","Accounts","Opportunities"]` | `self.config.get(...)` | Modules `sync()` projects into the KB |
| `rate_limit_per_min` | number | no | 60 | `self.rate_limit_per_min` | Client-side soft cap |

### Class constants (provider-wide)

```python
CONNECTOR_TYPE       = "sugarcrm"
CONNECTOR_NAME       = "SugarCRM"
AUTH_TYPE            = "oauth2_password"
REQUIRED_CONFIG_KEYS = ["site_url"]
_STATUS_MAP = {
    401: ("OFFLINE",   "TOKEN_EXPIRED"),
    403: ("UNHEALTHY", "INVALID_CREDENTIALS"),
    429: ("DEGRADED",  "CONNECTED"),
}
```

sections.title: API Methods
sections.content: All public async methods on `SugarCRMConnector`. SOC: orchestration only — every HTTP call delegates to `client/http_client.py::SugarCRMHTTPClient`.

children.title: Lifecycle
children.content:
### `async install() -> InstallResult`
Validate `site_url`; for the password grant, exchange `username` + `password` for tokens and call `set_token`. For the authorization_code grant, return success and wait for `authorize()`.

### `async authorize(auth_code, state) -> TokenInfo`
Exchange an authorization code for `access_token` + `refresh_token` (authorization_code grant only).

### `async health_check() -> HealthCheckResult`
`GET /me` — lightweight identity probe. Classifies failures by `_STATUS_MAP`.

### `async sync(since=None, full=False, kb_id=None, webhook_url=None) -> SyncResult`
Page every module in `sync_modules` (default `Contacts` + `Accounts` + `Opportunities`) using `offset / max_num`. Apply `?filter=[{"date_modified":{"$gt":since}}]` when `since` is set and `full=False`. Normalise each record via `helpers.normalizer.normalize_record(...)` and call `ingest_document`.

children.title: Contacts
children.content:
### `async list_contacts(offset=0, max_num=50, filter=None) -> Dict`
`GET /Contacts` — returns `{records: [...], next_offset: N|-1}`. `filter` is a SugarCRM predicate list (JSON-encoded into a single querystring value).

### `async get_contact(contact_id) -> Dict`
`GET /Contacts/{id}`.

### `async create_contact(first_name, last_name, email=None, phone_work=None) -> Dict`
`POST /Contacts`. `email` is rendered as the SugarCRM `email` array with `primary_address=true` so the value lands on the contact's default email slot.

### `async update_contact(contact_id, fields: Dict) -> Dict`
`PUT /Contacts/{id}` — arbitrary field bag.

### `async delete_contact(contact_id) -> Dict`
`DELETE /Contacts/{id}`.

children.title: Accounts · Opportunities · Leads
children.content:
### `async list_accounts(offset=0, max_num=50) -> Dict`
`GET /Accounts`.

### `async list_opportunities(offset=0, max_num=50) -> Dict`
`GET /Opportunities`.

### `async create_opportunity(name, account_id=None, amount=0, sales_stage="Prospecting", date_closed=None) -> Dict`
`POST /Opportunities`. SugarCRM requires `date_closed` at the storage layer — when omitted, the connector defaults it to `today + 30 days`.

### `async list_leads(offset=0, max_num=50) -> Dict`
`GET /Leads`.

### `async convert_lead(lead_id, modules: Dict) -> Dict`
`POST /Leads/{id}/convert`. `modules` follows the SugarCRM convert payload shape:

```json
{
  "Contacts":      { "first_name": "...", "last_name": "..." },
  "Accounts":      { "name": "..." },
  "Opportunities": { "name": "...", "amount": 0 }
}
```

children.title: Meetings
children.content:
### `async list_meetings(offset=0, max_num=50) -> Dict`
`GET /Meetings`. Returns calendar meetings (one-time + recurring instances).

sections.title: Error Handling
sections.content:
All HTTP errors are translated by `client/http_client.py::SugarCRMHTTPClient._raise_for_status(...)` into a single typed hierarchy in `exceptions.py`:

| HTTP | Exception | Behaviour |
|---|---|---|
| 401 | `SugarCRMAuthError` | `helpers.utils.refresh_and_retry_on_401` triggers `on_token_refresh()` + single retry; a second 401 in the same call chain propagates |
| 403 | `SugarCRMError` | propagates; `health_check` classifies as `UNHEALTHY + INVALID_CREDENTIALS` |
| 404 | `SugarCRMError` | propagates to caller |
| 422 | `SugarCRMError` | propagates with the SugarCRM `error_message` body parsed into the exception |
| 429 | `SugarCRMRateLimitError(retry_after)` | `helpers.utils.with_retry` honours `Retry-After` on the first attempt then exponential backoff |
| 5xx | `SugarCRMNetworkError` | `with_retry` exponential backoff (max 3 retries, 32 s cap, jittered) |
| transport (DNS, connect, read timeout) | `SugarCRMNetworkError` | same retry policy as 5xx |

`with_retry`'s knobs (`RETRY_DELAY_S=1.0`, `BACKOFF_FACTOR=2.0`, `MAX_RETRY_DELAY_S=32.0`) live at the top of `helpers/utils.py` — OCP, tune in one place.

Error payloads typically arrive as:

```json
{ "error": "need_login", "error_message": "Token is invalid or expired" }
```

The HTTP client extracts the first of `error_message`, `message`, `error`, `error_description` for the human-readable exception message.

sections.title: Troubleshooting
sections.content:
### `install()` returns `success=False` with message `"SugarCRM rejected credentials"` The password grant token call returned 401. Causes: * Wrong `username` / `password`. * Wrong `client_id` for a custom OAuth application (try the default `sugar`). * The SugarCRM user is locked or inactive. * The site enforces SSO and disables the password grant. Verify with curl: ```bash curl -sS -X POST "https://acme.sugarondemand.com/rest/v11/oauth2/token" \ -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \ -d '{"grant_type":"password","client_id":"sugar","username":"","password":"

","platform":"api"}' ``` ### `health_check()` returns `healthy=false` * On 401 → tokens expired AND refresh failed AND fallback password grant was unavailable (`username`/`password` blank or invalid). Re-run install with fresh credentials. * On `RefreshError` → no `refresh_token` on file and no password-grant fallback. Re-install. * On 5xx → SugarCRM is down or upgrading; the connector already retried 3 times; check the SugarCloud status page. ### Every call returns 401 even immediately after install Wrong `platform` value. SugarCRM treats each `platform` as a separate session; if your tenant uses a custom platform string (e.g. `mobile`, `portal`) the `api` default will mint tokens but read paths will reject them. Set `platform` to match your SugarCRM platform registration. ### `convert_lead()` returns `{ "converted": false }` The `modules` envelope is mandatory. Pass: ```python await conn.convert_lead("lead-id", { "Contacts": {"first_name": "X", "last_name": "Y"}, "Accounts": {"name": "Acme"}, "Opportunities": {"name": "Deal", "amount": 10000, "sales_stage": "Prospecting"}, }) ``` ### `create_opportunity()` fails on `date_closed` SugarCRM requires `date_closed` at the storage layer. The connector defaults it to `today + 30d` when you omit it; if you supply it, use `YYYY-MM-DD` (not ISO datetime). ### Sync indexes zero documents * Check the SugarCRM service account has read access to the modules in `sync_modules`. * Check `since` is not in the future (filter `date_modified > since` will return zero rows). * Watch `structlog` for `sugarcrm.sync.page_failed` — that surfaces the underlying SugarCRM error verbatim.
